rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intermolecular sulfonylation/heteroarylation of alkenes (benzothiazole-substituted tertiary homoallylic alcohols) with sulfinic acids via distal heteroaryl ipso-migration, forming new C–C and C–S bonds.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfinate anion (from deprotonation of sulfinic acid) to a sulfonyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2; a second anodic SET oxidizes a ketyl radical intermediate to a carbocation.

key_intermediates.name: S-centered sulfonyl radical (III), resonance form of II
carbon radical from sulfonyl addition to alkene (IV)
spiro N-radical (V) from intramolecular ipso-cyclization onto benzothiazole
carbocation intermediate (VII) after anodic SET oxidation of VI

electron_flow: Sulfinic acid is deprotonated and the sulfinate is anodically oxidized (SET) to a sulfonyl radical, which adds across the alkene to give a C-radical; intramolecular ipso-addition onto the benzothiazole forms a spiro N-radical that undergoes C–C bond cleavage/ring-opening to a ketyl radical, which is anodically oxidized to a cation, then deprotonated to give the migrated ketone product. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (3a 0%), starting material 1a almost fully recovered — supports radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Sodium p-tolylsulfinate (4)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a in 65% yield, similar to sulfinic acid, supporting deprotonation/sulfinate as the active species.

probe_reagents.name: p-toluenesulfinic acid alone (no tertiary alcohol)
probe_reagents.observation: Homocoupling to 4,4'-dimethyldiphenyl disulfone (5) in 70% yield, indicating sulfonyl radical generation under electrolysis.

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a formed, confirming the transformation is electrochemically driven.

probe_reagents.name: Chain-length variation (1u–1x, n=0,1,3,4)
probe_reagents.observation: Only n=2 (1,4-mig.) and n=3 (1,5-mig.) gave product; 3-, 4-, and 7-membered cyclic TS were ineffective, supporting 5-/6-membered cyclic intramolecular migration via spirocyclic N-radical.

notes: X-ray structure of 3a (CCDC 1867118) confirms regiochemistry. Sulfonyl radicals II (O-centered) and III (S-centered) are described as resonance structures; S-centered form adds to alkene. Mechanism is intramolecular heteroaryl ipso-migration (Truce–Smiles-type) rather than intermolecular; supported by chain-length scan showing strict preference for 5-/6-membered spirocyclization.
